stories.title: Knicks 109-93 Cavaliers — ECF Game 2 (May 21) — Hart 26, Brunson 14 Ast, 2-0 Lead
stories.angle: Hart was the unexpected hero — balanced scoring, playmaking, floor-spreading — while Brunson ran the offense with surgical precision. Cleveland had no answers. ECF Game 3: May 23, Cleveland, 8 PM ET, ABC.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
JOSH HART: 26 POINTS (playoff career-high).
JALEN BRUNSON: 14 ASSISTS (playoff career-high).

Both. Same game.

Knicks 109, Cavaliers 93. New York leads ECF 2-0.

#HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Hart shot 5-of-11 from three. Brunson dished 14 dimes. Cleveland had no answers.

Knicks 109-93 in Game 2. They're two wins away from the NBA Finals.

What is stopping New York right now?

#HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Two playoff career-highs in the same game. That's what it took to run the Cleveland Cavaliers out of Madison Square Garden again.

Josh Hart scored 26 points — the best postseason performance of his career — going 10-of-21 from the field and 5-of-11 from three-point range while adding 7 assists, 4 rebounds, and 2 steals. Jalen Brunson chipped in 19 points and 14 assists, the most dimes he has ever dished in a single playoff game.

Together, they were unstoppable.

New York won 109-93 and now leads the Eastern Conference Finals 2-0 with Game 3 heading to Cleveland on Saturday. The Knicks are two wins away from the NBA Finals, and they are playing with the kind of confidence that comes from winning ugly (Game 1 comeback) and winning comfortably (Game 2 blowout) in back-to-back nights.

Cleveland's defense had no answer for the two-man game between Hart and Brunson. When they overloaded on Brunson, Hart made threes. When they played Hart straight up, Brunson found his way to the rim or freed up Karl-Anthony Towns in the post. The Cavaliers tried adjustments, and none of them worked.

For the Cavs, the situation is now mathematically desperate. No NBA team has ever come back from an 0-3 deficit. Donovan Mitchell and James Harden need a completely different performance on Saturday in Cleveland — and they need their home crowd to help them find it.

ECF Game 3: Saturday, May 23, Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse, Cleveland, 8 PM ET, ABC. Knicks lead series 2-0.
facebook.image_caption:
Hart: 26. Brunson: 14 assists. BOTH playoff career-highs in the same game.

Knicks 109, Cavaliers 93. New York leads the ECF 2-0.

This team is built for this moment. #HoopHeroes #Knicks

article.headline: Hart's 26, Brunson's 14 Assists Lead Knicks Past Cavaliers 109-93 in ECF Game 2

stories.title: WCF Game 3 TONIGHT — Series 1-1, Spurs Home, Massive Injury Cloud
stories.angle: The injury situation dramatically changes the WCF calculus. If Fox and Harper are out, San Antonio relies entirely on Wembanyama and Stephon Castle. Game 3 is effectively a must-win for the Spurs to keep series parity.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
TONIGHT. San Antonio. 8:30 PM ET. NBC/Peacock.

De'Aaron Fox (ankle). Dylan Harper (adductor). Jalen Williams (hamstring).

All three questionable for WCF Game 3.

Wembanyama carries the load — again.

#HoopHeroes #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
WCF Game 3 is LIVE. Series tied 1-1. AT&T Center packed.

Spurs could be short-handed. Wembanyama doesn't care.

This is the game that could define the whole series.

8:30 PM ET. NBC/Peacock.

#HoopHeroes #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
TONIGHT. San Antonio. 8:30 PM ET on NBC/Peacock. Western Conference Finals Game 3.

And the injury situation surrounding this game is unlike anything we've seen this deep in the playoffs in years.

De'Aaron Fox has not played a single minute of the Western Conference Finals. The Spurs' starting point guard has been sidelined since suffering a right high ankle sprain in the second round against Minnesota, and coach Mitch Johnson put it bluntly: "He's just trying to play every day. It's a tough injury that he wouldn't be playing with in the regular season."

Then Dylan Harper — the rookie who carried the Spurs in Fox's absence with 24 points and a franchise-record 7 steals in Game 1, and 12 points before exiting Game 2 — is now questionable himself with a right adductor strain suffered in the third quarter Wednesday.

And then there's Jalen Williams. The Thunder's second-best player exited Game 2 in the first quarter with left hamstring soreness. It's his fourth hamstring injury in five months. He traveled with OKC to San Antonio and will be evaluated game-to-game.

So here we are: WCF tied 1-1, Game 3 tonight, and we don't know exactly who is playing for either team.

What we do know is that Victor Wembanyama is healthy. He averaged 31 points and 20.5 rebounds in the first two games of this series. If the Spurs are going to hold serve at home with a short-handed roster, he will need every bit of that — and then some.

Game 3 TONIGHT: 8:30 PM ET, AT&T Center, San Antonio, NBC/Peacock. Series tied 1-1.
facebook.image_caption:
Fox. Harper. Williams. All questionable for WCF Game 3 TONIGHT.

Wembanyama vs. the Thunder in San Antonio. 8:30 PM ET NBC/Peacock.

This series just got a lot more complicated. #HoopHeroes #Spurs

article.headline: WCF Game 3 Tonight: Three Spurs Stars Questionable as Series Returns to San Antonio

stories.title: WCF Game 2 Recap — Thunder 122-113 Spurs (May 20) — SGA 30, Series Even
stories.angle: SGA's command of the game was decisive. But the story was the injuries: Williams exited in Q1, Harper in Q3 — clouding what comes next.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
SGA: 30 points. 9 assists. +14.

Thunder had 9 turnovers. Spurs had 21.

That's the WCF series in a nutshell. OKC tied it 1-1.

Game 3 TONIGHT in San Antonio. 8:30 PM ET.

#HoopHeroes #Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander would not let the Thunder fall behind 0-2. And he made sure everyone knew it.

SGA finished with 30 points, 9 assists, 2 blocks, and a +14 differential in Oklahoma City's 122-113 Game 2 win over San Antonio on Wednesday night at Paycom Center. He shot 50 percent from the field, attacked the paint when the Spurs sagged back, and found cutters when they ran him off the three-point line. It was a 48-minute masterclass.

But this game wasn't just about SGA. It was about turnovers. San Antonio committed 21 of them; OKC committed 9. That 12-possession advantage translated directly into OKC transition points that kept the Thunder comfortably in control.

Victor Wembanyama did everything he could — 21 points, 17 rebounds, playing the kind of game that makes you realize this person has no ceiling. Stephon Castle, already stepping in for the injured De'Aaron Fox, posted 28 points and 8 assists in a remarkable individual performance.

But the injuries haunted San Antonio again. Jalen Williams, OKC's second star, exited in the first quarter with left hamstring soreness and did not return. Dylan Harper left in the third quarter with a right leg issue later diagnosed as a right adductor strain.

When the final buzzer sounded, both teams were looking at injury reports instead of box scores.

Series tied 1-1. WCF Game 3: TONIGHT at AT&T Center, San Antonio, 8:30 PM ET, NBC/Peacock.
facebook.image_caption:
30 points. 9 assists. +14. SGA was locked in.

Thunder 122, Spurs 113. WCF tied 1-1. But the injuries are what everyone is really watching.

Game 3 TONIGHT in San Antonio. #HoopHeroes #Thunder

article.headline: Thunder 122, Spurs 113: SGA's 30-Point, 9-Assist Performance Evens WCF at 1-1

stories.title: ECF Game 3 Preview — Knicks at Cleveland, May 23, ABC
stories.angle: Cleveland's home crowd will be electric, but the Knicks have momentum, confidence, and a blueprint that worked twice. Cavs must fix the bench scoring deficit and prevent Hart/Brunson from running freely.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Cleveland is down 0-2. No team has ever come back from 0-3 in NBA history.

Tomorrow at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse. 8 PM ET. ABC.

The Cavs need Saturday to be different. #HoopHeroes #Cavaliers #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Cleveland Cavaliers wake up Saturday morning staring at 0-2 in the Eastern Conference Finals, heading home to host a Knicks team that has beaten them both times they've played this series.

No NBA team has ever rallied from an 0-3 deficit to win a playoff series. That's the cliff edge Cleveland is standing on if they lose Saturday at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse.

Donovan Mitchell needs to be the player he knows he can be — the one who put up 41 points in Game 7 against Detroit, the one who can carry a series on his back when the moment demands it. Mitchell scored well in Games 1 and 2, but the moments that defined both those games were controlled by Brunson. On Saturday, Mitchell needs to flip that equation.

James Harden was a problem in Game 1 — targeted defensively, coughing up six turnovers, shooting 1-of-8 from three. Game 2 was a step backward for the whole Cavaliers offense. At home, with the crowd behind them, the Cavs need Harden to recapture the playmaking control that made him an All-NBA performer this season.

For the Knicks, the challenge is simpler: don't let the environment rattle you. Brunson has been exceptional. Hart has been exceptional. Karl-Anthony Towns and Mikal Bridges have contributed. The offensive engine is running hot, and there's no reason to assume one road game will stop it.

ECF Game 3: Saturday May 23, Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse, Cleveland, 8 PM ET, ABC. Knicks lead series 2-0.
facebook.image_caption:
The Cavs need Game 3. Badly.

No team has ever come back from 0-3 in NBA history. Cleveland is one loss away from that cliff.

Game 3 TOMORROW night at home. 8 PM ET on ABC. #HoopHeroes #Cavaliers

stories.title: Josh Hart — Playoff Career-High 26 Points, The Heartbeat of the Knicks
stories.angle: Hart was a second-round pick who had to fight for his place in the league. Now he's delivering playoff career-highs in the Conference Finals.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Josh Hart. 30th pick. Playoff career-high 26 points in the ECF.

This is what the Knicks built. This is why no one in New York is worried.

Hart doesn't flinch. #HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
When the New York Knicks selected Josh Hart in a trade deadline move years ago, the analytics were fine and the fit made sense. Nobody was predicting he'd be posting playoff career-highs in the Eastern Conference Finals.

And yet there he was Thursday night — 26 points, 7 assists, 4 rebounds, 2 steals. Going 10-of-21 from the field, 5-of-11 from three. Doing it in a game where both he and Jalen Brunson set personal playoff career records on the same night.

Hart is the anti-star star. He doesn't dominate highlights or get in the MVP conversation. He sets screens, chases loose balls, sprints back on defense, and does every single thing a basketball team needs its third or fourth option to do — then turns around and drops 26 when the moment calls for it.

His three-point shooting evolution has been the key. At 47.6 percent from deep in this ECF, he has become a real floor-spacing threat, which forces Cleveland to make choices it doesn't want to make. Every time they cheat toward Brunson or Towns, Hart is there to punish them.

What's also remarkable: Hart did this in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Finals. In a building that was still buzzing from one of the greatest comebacks in recent NBA playoff history. After a game where the Knicks drained every ounce of energy from the building. Hart came back 48 hours later and played even better.

The Knicks have a lot of good players. Josh Hart is the heartbeat.
facebook.image_caption:
30th pick. Playoff career-high 26 points. Eastern Conference Finals.

Josh Hart is not a surprise to anyone who has watched this Knicks team — but the number on the scoreboard still makes you look twice.

That's just what he does. #HoopHeroes #Knicks

article.headline: Josh Hart's 26-Point Playoff Career-High Is the Knicks Story Nobody Saw Coming
